scope.summary: OracleConnection now requires explicit logical close implementations and the server shutdown/profile/pool lifecycle drives them instead of relying on drop.
out_of_scope: B7b pooled installed-service proof beyond the D7 stdio lane
driver source changes in the sibling rust-oracledb repo

entry_points.name: OracleConnection trait and thin adapter close path
entry_points.trace: Commit 2746b6c1 makes OracleConnection::close required instead of a default no-op, implements it across production/test backends, and RustOracleConnection delegates to the published driver's consuming Connection::close path.

entry_points.name: Pool, dispatcher, lane, and stdio shutdown lifecycle
entry_points.trace: Commit 2746b6c1 closes retired pool/dispatcher sessions; commit 7e20771b makes LaneRuntime and StatefulLaneDispatch close their owning lanes, exposes OracleMcpServer::close_blocking, and calls it on stdio EOF plus SIGTERM/SIGINT.

entry_points.name: cargo fmt --all -- --check
entry_points.trace: Passed after the B7c/D7 shutdown changes.

entry_points.name: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target cargo check -p oraclemcp-db
entry_points.trace: Passed after making OracleConnection::close required and explicit across the DB crate.

entry_points.name: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target cargo check -p oraclemcp-core && CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target cargo check -p oraclemcp
entry_points.trace: Both scoped checks passed after the lane/server/stdout shutdown close changes.

entry_points.name: scripts/swarm_discipline.sh bounded-run --timeout 300 -- scripts/rig/verify_logoff_lane.sh
entry_points.trace: Passed against rust-oracledb-free/FREEPDB1: clean EOF produced +1 logoff row, SIGKILL produced 0 rows as the abrupt-control, and SIGTERM produced +1 logoff row.

limitations: The D7 lane is a stdio/process SIGTERM proof against the existing Free23 container, not an installed systemd service run.
The live D7 lane drives the single stateful stdio session; broader pooled installed-service proof remains in B7b/D7 follow-on scope.
The full workspace gate was not run because this shared checkout has unrelated in-flight panes.
